Back to Goldcast

Posted Mar 22 2021-03-22T00:00:00+05:30 by Jeevan

Last week was no less than a roller coaster ride, I almost turned into a completely different person, I am not be blamed cheers to a sneaky playlist, got an interview call from Kivi Capital, joined back Goldcast as an intern, played heck lot of Valorant and finally, tweeted some really wierd stuff (about this in a different blog).

Search for internship

I was applying to many different startups in India, and was also getting reached out by them with the only problem being, every one of them required an assignment to be solved. Well that wouldn’t have been a problem if it was a normal week, but I also had sick tonne of college tests/assigments during the same week. I started solving a system design problem to deploy ML models, only to get psuedo-ghosted by the company, she would send a mail and expect me to join within 30 mins of the receiving that mail. I usually have no ego issues, but if somebody joins the meeting 20 mins late & doesn’t apologize and send emails expecting people to treat them like gods, you know, even I can be an A**hole. After that I started working on an assignment by a different org, this time it was to develop an API with given functionalities. It was nice but was draining a lot of time, I also was required to document and host it. I tried to solve as much as I can within 3 days and submitted it, this time I got ghosted. In the midst of all this, I was also in talk with Goldcast (last year I interned here) and finally decided to join there.

Interview call from Kivi Capital !

I applied to Kivi Capital on Instahyre, and to my surprise my Instahyre score was FULL, like the bar was complete indicating a very high match. A little context, Kivi Capital is a HFT firm, that pays ridiculously high, more than Google, Goldmansachs and definitely more than any other startup in India. So the CEO himself reached out to schedule an interview. At first I just agreed to whatever date he mentioned, which gave about 6 days time to prepare. Later that night after going through the job description, although I had every mentioned point that I am good at, I just felt that I ain’t my best at the moment. So here comes the gamble! The very next morning I mailed him regarding the same and said it would just be a waste of time if he interviwed me right now, and asked him would he do the same 3 months from now if the job opening still exists ? I don’t know, not only my future interview isn’t guranteed but I’m also risking my current interview. And luckily he agreed to it, assuring that my application will be taken forward directly in the month of July if the opening still exists.
Now why is this a big thing ? Because Kivi Capital wasn’t even open to my department (Aerospace) when it visited our campus during placements, and to top it off only single digit students were selected for interviews among CS/EE students.

That being said, there are also few other firms that I’ll be interviewing with for now. I guess acche din started!

Blog
internship interview
